REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS CIVIC CENTER HOTEL AND BALWANTSINH THAKOR'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS; REQUEST FOR MONETARY AND TERMINATING SACTIONS. THE MOTION TO COMPEL THE APPEARANCE AT DEPOSITION OF THE TWELVE INDIVIDUAL PLAINTIFF'S LISTED IN THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE INDIVIDUAL PLAINTIFFS LISTED IN THE MOTION MUST APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION AT A TIME AND PLACE AGREED UPON BY THE PARTIES, DEPOSITIONS TO BE HELD WITHIN 45 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR TERMINATION SANCTIONS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $1,000.00 AS TO EACH OF THE TWELVE PLAINTIFFS LISTED IN THE MOTION IS ALSO GRANTED, PAYABLE WITHIN 45 DAYS OF THIS ORDER'S ENTRY IF THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION. ALTERNATIVELY, TO AVOID SANCTIONS THE PLAINTIFF MAY VOLUNTARILY DISMISS HIS OR HER CLAIMS WITHIN THE 45 DAY PERIOD. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES)ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Progress Report On The Status Of The Deposit Of Money Into A Blocked Account
Off Calendar Apr-10-2018 Off Calendar

Petition To Approve Compromise Of Pending Action As To Plaintiff Abraham Medina-Diaz, A Minor
Off Calendar Feb-22-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Pending Action of the Minor, Abraham Medina-Diaz.
Dept. 514. Judge: Cynthia Ming-Mei Lee. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Mark Hooshmand, Esq. appeared with petitioner Sandra Diaz, who was present as Guardian ad Litem for the minor Abraham Medina-Diaz, who was also present at this morning's hearing.
Petitioner and the minor were placed under oath and testified.
Order appointing Ms. Diaz Guardian ad Litem for the minor was filed on December 14, 2017.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the compromise is approved as follows:

Settlement Amount - $32,772.87.
Attorney Fees - $7,666.67.
Medical Bills - $795.45.
Costs - $2,106.20.

The net balance of $22,204.55 shall be deposited into a blocked account with Citibank, located at 451 Montgomery Street, San Francisco, CA 94104. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order or until the minors reach the age of 18 years.

Orders Approving the Compromise of a Pending Action of a Minor, and to Deposit Money Into a Blocked Account, were submitted to and signed by the Court.

Matter is CONTINUED to April 12, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for Progress Report on the Status of the deposit of money into a Blocked Account.

The Court announces the tentative ruling and reasons for it; statute of limitations has elapsed. The Court has accepted arguments by moving party. Both sides ARGUED. Plaintiff argues moving party did not shift burden. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Plaintiff's request to continue to file a supplemental pleading is Denied. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT U.A. LOCAL 38 PENSION TRUST FUND's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant U.A. Local 38 Pension Trust Funds Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication As To Some Or All Causes Of Action Asserted By Plaintiffs is GRANTED as requested as to certain Plaintiffs and certain causes of action based on the applicable statues of limitations per CCP 335.1 and CCP 340(a). Motion for summary judgment is framed by the allegations in the operative complaint. Moving party has shifted its burden and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact. Moving party to prepare order delineating MSJ/MSA as to each Plaintiff and each cause of action subject to this motion. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiffs April Campbell, Michael Bennett, Shawn Buckmaster, Joseph Catone, And Terri Jones
Off Calendar Nov-25-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JOSHUA BART, ESQ. of Low, Ball & Lynch, Tel No. 415-981-6630 appearing for Defendant Civic Center Hotel, LLC and Balwantsing Thakor;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - Defense counsel stipulates to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS CIVIC CENTER HOTEL LLC and BALWANTSINH THAKOR's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiffs April Campbell, Michael Bennett, Shawn Buckmaster, Joseph Catone, And Terri Jones, counsel for CIVIC CENTER HOTEL LLC and BALWANTSINH THAKOR are admonished for failure to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, a violation of Local Rule 2.6. The motion of Defendants CIVIC CENTER HOTEL LLC and BALWANTSINH THAKOR for terminating sanctions against Plaintiffs APRIL CAMPBELL, MICHAEL BENNETT, SHAWN BUCKMASTER, JOSEPH CATONE, AND TERRI JONES is GRANTED. Each of the Plaintiffs named in this motion have failed to appear for their depositions on at least two occasions and failed to comply with an Order from this Court dated September 18, 2015 compelling their attendance for deposition within 30 days. Plaintiffs APRIL CAMPBELL, MICHAEL BENNETT, SHAWN BUCKMASTER, JOSEPH CATONE, AND TERRI JONES has each exhibited repeated, willful, gross indifference to the responsibilities of being a party to a lawsuit and have obstructed the efforts of Defendants to obtain necessary pre-trial discovery. (See, Lang v. Hochman (2000) 77 CalApp4th 1225,1246) The claims of Plaintiffs APRIL CAMPBELL, MICHAEL BENNETT, SHAWN BUCKMASTER, JOSEPH CATONE, AND TERRI JONES are hereby dismissed with prejudice. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions against moving party Defendants is DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS PAUL GALINDO, BRNDON JEREZ, JEFFREY MORRIS, LORI MORRIS, CRAIG WENNIHAN, CHRIS MILLS, APRIL CAMPBELL, PAULA BUCKLEY, JOHN ADAMS, JENNIFER HOOPER, BARRY WILDER, JOSEPH CATONE, DAVID GEORGE, CLARENCE PERKINS, MARIA OLGUIN, BELINDA CAMPOS, JOHN STEWART, KOZERA MCFADDEN's Motion To Allow Postings In The Civic Center Hotel Per Local Ordinance And For Copies Of Notices. Plaintiffs move for a "preliminary injunction" to allow "postings" on the Defendants' property. The Motion is DENIED for two reasons: (1) the papers do not make the showings required for a preliminary injunction; (2) the "postings" as issue are expressly solicitations for employment by Plaintiffs' attorneys, for which Plaintiffs cite no authority. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Order is signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Joanna Broadwell, CSR#10959, (415-274-9977), Reported (501).

Court announces its tentative ruling in the beginning of the hearing as follows: Counsel for plaintiffs and defendants are ordered to meet and confer regarding solutions to the pest control problems. These solutions may include, but are not limited to, effective professional pest control and communications with residents regarding pest abatement, such as proper housekeeping and not leaving pet food out for vermin to. A joint statement regarding the meet and confer is to be filed with the Court on or before December 31, 2014. The motion for preliminary injunction is otherwise DENIED.

Both counsel briefly responds to the tentative ruling and submits to it. Court adopts the tentative ruling: PLAINTIFFS APRIL CAMPBELL, PAULA BUCKLEY, JOHN ADAMS, JOSEPH CATONE and BELINDA CAMPOS' Motion For Preliminary Injunction Against The Owners And Operators Of The Civic Center Hotel is DENIED. Court orders counsel for plaintiffs and defendants to meet and confer regarding solutions to the pest control problems. A joint statement regarding the meet and confer is to be filed with the Court on or before December 31, 2014. The motion for preliminary injunction is otherwise DENIED. Moving party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Reporter Francine Dais, CSR #8855, (916-446-2700), D501.
